name.description: Name of the Nth participating corporation in a nonprofit consolidation. Indexed list — supports 2+ parties (page 0 shows 2 inline; additional parties go on attached exhibits). Mirrors the merger.parties[N].* pattern from MNPCA-10.

descriptor.description: Descriptor/type adjective for the Nth participating corp, fills the '(A ___ Corporation)' slot. Examples: 'Maine non-profit', 'Delaware non-profit', 'New Hampshire'. Distinct from .name (which is the legal name) and from .governing_law_state (which applies only to the new entity).

adoption_method.description: Mutually-exclusive enum capturing how the Nth participating corp adopted the plan of consolidation: 'majority_member_vote' | 'supermajority_member_vote' | 'written_consent_of_members' | 'board_of_directors_majority_vote'. Per 13-B MRSA §906 each domestic party adopts separately. Form supports only one inline FOURTH block; additional domestic parties' adoption manners go on attached exhibits.

adoption_date.description: Date associated with the selected adoption_method (meeting date for options 1/2/4, written-consent date for option 3). Single key — only the widget paired (by y-proximity) with the selected method's checkbox is filled.

adoption_percentage.description: Percentage-of-member-votes value for the supermajority adoption_method (option 2). Required when adoption_method='supermajority_member_vote'. NOTE: the AcroForm widget for this value is missing on this template (template gap; see open_questions).

registered_office_address_line{1,2}.description: Two-line registered office address of the Nth participating corp in Maine (FIFTH section). Foreign parties use principal-place-of-business if they have no Maine registered office (per page-1 footnote).

date_signed.description: DATED line for the Nth party's signature block on page 1.

corporation_name.description: Re-statement of the Nth party's name on the DATED row of its signature block (right column).

printed_name_and_capacity.description: Printed name + capacity of the authorized officer who signs for the Nth party (e.g., 'Jane Smith, President').

name.description: Name of the NEW corporation formed by the consolidation. Distinct from merger pattern (where there is a 'surviving' corp) — consolidation produces a brand-new entity. Multiple widgets bind to this key (page 0 fields 5 and 8).

descriptor.description: Descriptor adjective for the new corporation's '(A ___ Corporation)' slot.

governing_law_state.description: State whose laws govern the new consolidated corporation.

service_address.description: Address to which SOS shall mail process when the new corp is governed by laws other than Maine.

foreign_jurisdictions.description: State(s) whose laws permit the foreign participating corporation(s) to consolidate (FIRST clause).

plan_exhibit_letter.description: Letter of the exhibit containing the plan of consolidation (THIRD).

effective_date.description: Optional future effective date of the consolidation (capped at 60 days post-filing per 13-B MRSA §906).

name.description: (A ___ Corporation) — first participating corp name (top recital)

adoption_method.description: FOURTH option 1: [ ] By the members at a meeting on (date) ___ at which a quorum was present and such plan received at least a majority of the votes which members were entitled to cast.

adoption_date.description: FOURTH option 1 date: ...at a meeting on (date) ___

registered_office_address_line1.description: FIFTH (domestic): ...is: ___ (inline trailing blank ending the sentence)

registered_office_address_line2.description: FIFTH (domestic): ___ (street, city, state and zip code)

corporation_name.description: (name of corporation) — signature block 1, right column

printed_name_and_capacity.description: (type or print name and capacity) — signature block 1

entities.description: Entity name(s) the filing covers, as listed on the cover block.
